description_long: This triumphal arch features five evenly spaced arches supported by six robust columns, each with a square base and simple capitals. The arches are semi-circular and the upper portion of the structure is highlighted by a contrasting color, suggesting decorative stonework or brick. The columns and archways are rendered in a light stone color, while the arch moldings are a warm terracotta hue, giving the structure a classical yet inviting appearance. The overall design is symmetrical, with no visible decorative reliefs or inscriptions, emphasizing structural clarity over ornamentation. The arch is relatively thin in depth, indicating it is more of a decorative or commemorative structure than a massive gateway. Its proportions and style evoke Roman architectural influences, suitable for placement in plazas, parks, or as a focal point in urban environments.
description: A symmetrical triumphal arch with five semi-circular arches supported by six light stone columns and terracotta-colored arch moldings, evoking a classical Roman style.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five arches and six columns, with the light stone and terracotta colors clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin depth of the structure and the profile of a single column and arch.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the terracotta arch moldings more prominent and the same column arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the arch and the profile of a single column.
